meta.source: The ART of Trading - Bennett A. McDowell

art_pie.description: Four slices required for trading success — master all four

slices.name: Risk Capital
slices.description: Only trade with money you can afford to lose
slices.application: Use dedicated risk capital separate from living expenses; emotional trading increases when capital is irreplaceable

slices.name: Trading and Investing System
slices.description: A defined, repeatable methodology for entries, exits, and risk
slices.application: ART provides structure via signals (P/MP pyramid points, 1B/2B reversal bars); customize to your style

slices.name: Money Management
slices.description: Position sizing, stop-loss placement, and portfolio risk limits
slices.application: Calculate trade size from stop distance; never risk more than 1-2% per trade; use ATR-based stops

slices.name: Psychology
slices.description: Trader mindset, discipline, and emotional control
slices.application: Obtain the trader's mind-set before live trading; align personality with chosen system via ART Profile

core_concepts.principle: No One Can Predict Markets Consistently
core_concepts.description: Markets cannot be predicted with true consistency — accept uncertainty
core_concepts.application: Focus on reacting to current price action rather than forecasting
common_mistakes: Holding losing positions based on original thesis
Ignoring current market reality for opinions

core_concepts.description: Look at what the market is doing now, not theories about what will happen
core_concepts.application: Use price and volume as undistorted truth; ART confirms or denies entries based on live data
common_mistakes: Trading on news distortion or exaggeration
Panic-selling on poor earnings when stock rallies
Following commentator opinions over chart data

core_concepts.principle: The Market Tells You How to Trade
core_concepts.description: Price and volume communicate entry/exit if you know how to listen
core_concepts.application: Let ART signals (pyramid points, reversal bars) guide decisions rather than forcing trades
common_mistakes: Forcing trades when no signal exists
Overriding system signals with gut feeling

core_concepts.description: Risk control is non-negotiable for long-term survival
core_concepts.application: Always set stop-loss before entry; size positions from stop distance; use ATR for volatility-adjusted stops
common_mistakes: Using arbitrary dollar stops instead of market-structure stops

core_concepts.description: Psychological readiness is prerequisite for any system to work
core_concepts.application: Paper-trade until consistent; develop discipline; accept losses as cost of business
common_mistakes: Going live before mastering paper trading

core_concepts.description: Your unique personality must match your trading style
core_concepts.application: Use ART Profile exercise to determine trend vs scalp vs countertrend preference
common_mistakes: Scalping when personality suits position trading

trend_trading.description: Trade in direction of established trend using ART pyramid points
trend_trading.application: Enter on P (pyramid) signals in trend direction; use higher-timeframe filter; trail stops

countertrend_trading.description: Trade reversals at extremes using 1B/2B reversal bar signals
countertrend_trading.application: Only countertrend at clear support/resistance with reversal bar confirmation; tighter stops

scalping.description: Quick in-and-out trades on minor signals within larger trend
scalping.application: Use minor pyramid points and 1B signals on lower timeframes; strict time stops

winning_mindset.description: Be forever skeptical — when you think the market must go one way, it often goes the opposite
winning_mindset.application: Maintain humility; use stops; never assume certainty about direction

master_trader_skills.principle: Six Essential Skills of Master Traders
master_trader_skills.description: Discipline, patience, adaptability, risk management, continuous learning, emotional control
master_trader_skills.application: Review trades weekly; journal decisions; adapt strategy to regime changes
common_mistakes: Same parameters in all market conditions

confirmation_bias.description: Seeking information that confirms existing beliefs
confirmation_bias.mitigation: Actively look for contradicting evidence before entering

loss_aversion.description: Holding losers too long, cutting winners too short
loss_aversion.mitigation: Predefined stops and targets; never move stop against position

recency_bias.description: Overweighting recent events in decision making

greater_fool_theory.description: Buying at any price assuming someone will pay more
greater_fool_theory.mitigation: Always assess value via technical structure and risk-reward
